locations.name: The Baron's estate
locations.description: Crafting evocative OSE-style manor descriptionA modest stone manor crowned with a small watchtower, furnished with simple elegance and adorned with select artworks rather than ostentatious wealth. An adjoining guesthouse stands nearby, while the main residence houses all necessary chambers for living and receiving

detailpage.name: The Baron's estate

The family.Baron Uskewitz Coldheart (Oos-ke-fits): A regal patriarch of some sixty years, the Baron serves as Snakefat's ultimate judge and arbiter while delegating daily governance to his appointed mayor, Tomas Delveson.
The family.Lady Mirema (Mir-Ma) Coldheart: The Baron's grey-haired wife, once striking and stylish, now serves as the town's informal mediator while bearing the visible weight of grief from losing her son to the sea.
The family.Sir Kaelen (Kay-lin) Coldheart: The Baron's firstborn heir and captain of the Darkspire, lost when the ship vanished at sea. His loss is a source of deep sorrow for the family and town.
The family.Lady Thessia (Tes-ya) Coldheart: The Baron's only daughter and second-born child, gifted with the magical spark of Wind and Air, was trained by the mage Declan before Tolvjen to study at the Elemental College of Borussa.
The family.Sir Garric (Gary-k) Coldheart: The Baron's youngest son, a tall and lean intelligence of twenty-four years with scholarly training, oversees the town's trade and manages the export of Fathomwyrm goods.
The family.Sir Maciten (Mace-ten) Eldran: Lady Mirema's cousin and fifth son of his own noble house, he serves as keeper of the town records and remains a constant aide to Garric, rarely seen apart from him.

detailpage.Description: The Baron's estate is a modest stone manor that serves as the residence of the town's leader, the Baron. The manor is crowned with a small watchtower, providing a vantage point to oversee the town and its surroundings. The interior of the manor is furnished with simple elegance, featuring select artworks that reflect the Baron's appreciation for culture without being ostentatious. An adjoining guesthouse stands nearby, ready to accommodate visitors or guests of the Baron. The main residence includes all necessary chambers for living and receiving guests, making it a central hub for the town's social and political activities.
detailpage.Recent Events: Since the disappearance of the Darkspire and the loss of Sir Kaelen, the Baron's estate has been a place of mourning and reflection for the family. Baron Uskewitz has become more reclusive, and Lady Mirema while still trying to maintain her composure, has been deeply affected by the loss of her son. The estate has also become a place where the town's leaders gather to discuss matters of governance and strategy, especially in light of the challenges posed by the Fathomwyrm and the town's reliance on its resources. The sour and depressive atmosphere of the estate has been palpable, with the family struggling to cope with their grief while trying to maintain their responsibilities to the town. The whole town feels the weight of the Baron's loss, and it has cast a shadow over the community, affecting morale and the overall atmosphere of Snakefat.

images.caption: The Baron's family portrait, depicting Baron Uskewitz, Lady Mirema and all their children.
The Baron's estate, a modest stone manor with an adjoining guesthouse.
A loosely based interpretation of the interior of the Baron's estate, showcasing its simple elegance and select artworks. This is not a correct map

locations.name: Militia house
locations.description: Elderly Thilma manages reception duties, the facilities include a bathing room, clothing stores, and a small holding cell for drunken locals. A massive taxidermied owlbear—slain by the captain's ancestor — stands in the guardhouse as a venerated relic. Captain Alric lives on premise

locations.name: Druidic Stones
locations.description: Ancient standing stones ring the outskirts of town, their surfaces moss-covered and etched with symbols whose purpose has been lost to time. Local children dare each other to touch them after dark.

locations.name: The Wyrm yard (Workshop)
locations.description: A reinforced timber hall reeking of salt, blood, smoke, and blubber, with drainage grooves carved into floors perpetually slick with offal and lined with hooks, boilers, and sharpening stones. Staffed by scarred veterans and apprentices, the hall is currently closed as there is no Fathomwyrm to process, but the smell lingers in the air and the tools remain ready for the next catch.

locations.name: Oil Hall (Storage)
locations.description: A sturdy stone and timber warehouse and silo stands nearly emptied of its Fathomwyrm stores—only three barrels of oil, salted meat, scales, and massive bones remain before a trade caravan arrives at week's end to restock. Sergeant Seemaus, a loyal elderly guardian given the post by the Baron, sleeps within and keeps a bell at hand to raise the alarm.

locations.name: The Wick & Hook (General Store)
locations.description: Run by sharp-eyed widow Gulda, reeks of lamp oil, fish-gut soap, and damp rope—its shelves stocked with ropes, fishing spears, crossbows, oilskins, and rations, all arranged beneath the harpoon she claims gave the legendary One-Eye Snake its name. Goods are supplied without question or refund.

locations.name: The Grainhold
locations.description: The Old Church is a historic place of worship that has stood for centuries. It is known for its stunning architecture and rich history, making it a must-visit location for tourists and locals alike.

locations.name: Chapel of Foam and Ice
locations.description: A seaside shrine dedicated to an Elemental Seadrake, where ritual offerings are made before ships depart and Father Delm—a middle-aged, intense priest—preaches the sea's duality of nourishment and destruction during sermons held fortnightly or upon sailors' request.

locations.name: The Adventurer's Ale
locations.description: Newly built and fastly gaining popularity, this tavern is frequented by adventurers and locals alike. Known for its hearty meals, strong drinks, and lively atmosphere, it is a place where stories are shared and plans are made. It also helps that it is the only tavern and inn in town, making it a central hub for socializing and lodging.

locations.name: The Old leaning tower
locations.description: An ancient stone tower tilts precariously to one side, now the home of Tolvjen, an elder water-mage with a long white beard. Dismissed from the tutelage of the Baron's daughter he now pursues his own magical theories. His cluttered study overflows with half-finished experiments and enchanted curiosities, and though absent-minded and sarcastic, he remains a patient—if distracted—instructor in the arcane arts.

locations.name: The twin farms
locations.description: Small plots tended by extended families under the Baron's license yield onions, barley, and buttergreens alongside herds of chickens, goats, and pigs—providing a fifth of the town's provisions despite the region's meager soil.
